SafetyConnect · Design System
Case Study / Lead Designer

Scaling with purpose: a design system built for a safety mission.

How a scalable, component-based design system powered SafetyConnect's mission to reduce field-force incidents and drive adoption across geographies and industries.

Drop in accidents within six months

01 About SafetyConnect

An AI-powered suite that safeguards the field force.

SafetyConnect is an AI-powered software suite designed to safeguard field-force employees, particularly those in transportation and logistics roles. It combines action tracking, continuous driving-behavior analysis, and suggestive behavior changes to help large enterprises reduce accidents and promote safer working conditions.

/ 01

Action Tracking

Monitoring safety-critical actions for greater visibility.

/ 02

AI-Driven Behavior Analysis

Identifying risky driving patterns and potential hazards with the proprietary SafeDrive model.

/ 03

Real-time Feedback

Giving field workers actionable insights to foster safer decision-making.

/ 04

Data-Driven Insights

Empowering companies to proactively optimize safety programs and track overall progress.

02 Target Audience

Who the system serves.

Direct Users

Field-Force EmployeesDrivers, delivery personnel, technicians and field-service workers using the mobile app to track safety behaviors and receive real-time feedback.
Safety Managers / AdministratorsResponsible for overseeing safety programs, analyzing data from the web platform, and implementing corrective actions.

Indirect Users

Executives & Decision-MakersC-suite and business leaders who rely on SafetyConnect's insights to evaluate safety performance, make strategic investments, and support compliance reporting.
03 Challenges

A framework that couldn't keep pace with growth.

Problem statement. SafetyConnect needed a scalable, efficient design system to support rapid expansion across geographies and industries. The existing framework was not optimized for the pace and scale of feature rollout the platform demanded.

User needs. Field workers and safety managers needed clear navigation, intuitive workflows and understandable data presentation to build trust and adoption — while the platform had to adapt to diverse UX needs across industries, countries and potential language barriers.

Business goals. Enhance scalability and operational efficiency through a well-defined design system: faster feature development, less redundancy, and tighter collaboration between designers and developers — ultimately lifting user satisfaction and engagement.

Key Identifiable Issues
ISSUE 01

No scalable design system

Inconsistent UI patterns made it hard to roll out updates, onboard clients and maintain UX quality as the platform grew.

ISSUE 02

UX friction

Interface complexity reduced satisfaction and trust, hindering adoption and the platform's effectiveness.

ISSUE 03

Operational inefficiency

Unclear design–development handoffs led to redundancy, miscommunication and slower time-to-market.

04 My Role & Objectives

Leading an end-to-end UX strategy for growth.

As Lead Designer, my mission was to spearhead the transformation of SafetyConnect's digital product experience and establish an end-to-end UX strategy to facilitate the company's rapid growth.

  1. SaaS & Mobile App Revamp

    Overhaul the experience of both the web platform and mobile app, prioritizing intuitive navigation and clear presentation of safety data.

  2. Scalable Design System

    Craft a comprehensive, component-based system on Atomic Design principles to streamline development, ensure consistency and accelerate iteration.

  3. Enhanced UX

    Elevate the experience across all touchpoints, fostering satisfaction, trust and widespread adoption of the platform.

  4. End-to-end UX Strategy

    Develop a holistic strategy aligning the design and evolution of the products with user needs, business goals and the company's mission.

05 Process

Lean UX, structured by Atomic Design.

To navigate the redesign and establish a robust design system, I merged the Lean UX process with Atomic Design principles — chosen for adaptability, an emphasis on user validation, and efficiency in development.

APPROACH A

Atomic Design for scalability

Built a scalable, foundational system that keeps the UI consistent while enabling rapid development. The UI was broken into atoms, molecules and organisms — a modular language that supports easy adaptation. A major focus on tokenizing color, spacing and typography streamlined the design-to-development handoff.

APPROACH B

Lean UX process

Exploring problems (Think), creating solutions (Make) and validating with users (Check) kept the system and its components quickly tested, refined and aligned with user needs and business goals — avoiding redundancy so each component served a distinct purpose.

06 Solution

The SafetyConnect Design System.

Color styles with accessibility considerations built in — plus space, grid and breakpoint systems and typography — formed the Foundations. Atoms such as buttons, input fields, toggles and switches followed. Molecules like cards and dashboard widgets came next, then organisms, templates and pages.

FOUNDATION

Accessibility-first color tokens

accent/600
success/700
warning/500
info/600
ink/900
ink/500
FOUNDATION

07 Results & Impact

Substantial gains across experience, delivery and business.

User Experience Metrics
−30%

Task completion

Incident-reporting flow time reduced.

+25%

Time on task

Dashboard insights comprehension improved.

−70%

Error reduction

Incorrect action-tracking entries decreased.

Development Efficiency Gains
20–30%

Faster delivery

Shortened design-to-code cycles for quicker rollouts.

15–20%

Less rework

Fewer bugs and less back-and-forth between design and dev.

+10%

Collaboration

Smoother workflows boosting developer satisfaction.

Business Impact
/ growth

Supported rapid growth

Scalable UX let SafetyConnect onboard new clients efficiently and adapt to their needs.

/ perception

Enhanced client perception

Positive stakeholder feedback signalled a more user-friendly, effective product.

/ mission

Mission alignment

Furthered the goal of reducing workplace incidents and fostering a safety-first culture.

08 Conclusion & Key Learnings

Design as a driver of mission and growth.

Implementing a scalable design system alongside an overhaul of core experiences significantly improved user satisfaction, streamlined development, and positioned the company for accelerated growth.

LEARNING 01

Prioritize user-centricity

A deep understanding of user needs and pain points guided the success of the redesign.

LEARNING 02

Strategic design systems

Investing in a well-structured system is fundamental to scaling design and development efficiently.

LEARNING 03

Collaborative approach

Strong alignment between design and development was vital to maximizing impact.

The success at SafetyConnect demonstrates the power of design to drive tangible improvements in user experience, operational efficiency, and ultimately, to advance a company's mission within its industry.

End of case study
